input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest SDK version
Invoke-RestMethod https://dotnetcli.blob.core.windows.net/dotnet/release-metadata/releases-index.json |
  Select-Object -ExpandProperty 'releases-index' | Where-Object { $_.'support-phase' -eq 'active' }

# Install via winget (preferred)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8  # or .9 based on discovery above
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Android SDK Components

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK basics, but you may need to manage versions:

```powershell
# Find where Android SDK was installed
$androidSdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
# Or check:
dotnet android sdk info

# Discover installed/available API levels
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific platform & build tools (example: API 34)
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" `
  "build-tools;34.0.0" `
  "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 4. Set Up Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# Install system image and create AVD
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API_34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

## 5. Enable Windows App SDK Target

```powershell
# Windows App SDK comes with the maui workload, but ensure Developer Mode is on:
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"
# Toggle "Developer Mode" to ON in the UI that opens

# Verify Windows target is available
dotnet new maui --list
```

## 6. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" | Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName,
  "User")
```

## 7. Verify Everything Works

```powershell
# Create and build a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest -o ./MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0
```

## 8. Dynamic Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

```powershell
# Discover target framework versions your SDK supports
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet workload list

# Discover Android API levels in your project
grep -i "TargetFramework" *.csproj

# Find installed JDK (MAUI bundles one)
Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*"
```

---

**Key notes:**
- The `maui` workload pulls in `android`, `ios` (unused here), and Windows App SDK dependencies automatically.
- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` throughout if your SDK discovery in step 1 shows .NET 9 as the active release.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for fast Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
